title: Inventory census migration, satellite environmental, economic, and survey data sources for South Asian migration-environment analysis
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates census migration data (definition, frequency, coverage), satellite environmental indicators (NDVI, CHIRPS, flood data), economic surveys (employment, income), and population data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit cross-country comparability assessment.
inputs: National census migration-question documentation for South Asian countries
MODIS NDVI and CHIRPS rainfall product documentation
NSS and equivalent employment/income survey documentation
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, migration-definition comparability, and environmental-indicator methodology.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering migration-definition comparability, temporal resolution, and cross-country harmonization.

title: Compile harmonized district-level migration flow data for at least two South Asian countries
outcome: A harmonized district-level migration-flow dataset for at least two South Asian countries, documenting census migration definitions used, harmonization methodology, and cross-country comparability limitations.
inputs: Census migration data for target countries
Administrative boundary data for district-level aggregation
expected_artifact: validation.md update with migration-data methodology and comparability assessment.
validation_method: Replicator confirms census data extraction, definition harmonization methodology, and comparability documentation for each target country.
done_condition: Migration dataset includes methodology, definition documentation, harmonization approach, and replication status for at least two countries.

title: Correlate satellite-derived environmental change with census migration flows with economic confound controls
outcome: A correlation analysis linking NDVI trends, rainfall anomalies, and flood frequency to census migration flows at district scale, with explicit economic-confound controls, variance-explained documentation, and non-causal attribution caveats.
inputs: MODIS NDVI trends for target districts
CHIRPS rainfall anomalies for target periods
economic control variables (employment, income, agricultural productivity)
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with correlation methodology, confound controls, and non-causal caveats.
validation_method: Replicator confirms correlation methodology, economic-confound controls, variance-explained documentation, and spatial-autocorrelation handling.
done_condition: Correlation analysis includes methodology, economic confound controls, variance-explained (5-15 percent typical), non-causal caveats, and replication status.

title: Red-team climate-migration attribution overstatement and mobility-restriction risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how environmental-migration correlations could overstate climate causation, how migration maps could be used to restrict mobility rather than support adaptation, and how the adaptive-vs-distress distinction could be lost in policy communication.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every correlation claim has economic-confound controls, variance-explained documentation, and non-causal caveats. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every climate-migration claim is either blocked, softened to correlation-limited language with explicit economic confound and non-causal caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match urban-planning and adaptation-policy decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the environmental-migration correlation analysis would change any real decision for urban planners, adaptation policymakers, or district administrators without creating perverse incentives.
inputs: urban planning context for destination cities
adaptation policy context for origin districts
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for urban-planning and adaptation audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that migration maps are used to restrict mobility rather than support adaptation, or that modest correlations are presented as climate causation. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Dhaka city urban planner), named decision (e.g., service provision planning for climate-origin migrants), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for mobility restriction, not for climate-attribution claims without economic confound controls), and replicated field-reality review.
